summary: Excellent soundboard recording of an iconic Alpine Valley 7/17/89 show – crystal‑clear mix and a blistering set highlighted by a monster China>Rider and soaring Row Jimmy.
review: The GD‑89‑07‑17 SBD captures one of the most celebrated nights of the late‑80s run at Alpine Valley. Reviewers rave about the pristine, well‑balanced sound (vocals out front, instruments clear) and describe a show that burns from the first “Let the Good Times Roll” into an unforgettable second set – China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, Row Jimmy, Built to Last, Not Fade Away, The Music Never Stopped and the historic “We Bid You Goodnight.” Jerry’s lead‑guitar work is described as “on fire,” Brent’s organ shines, and the rhythm section drives with relentless energy.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 reviews, virtually all five‑star, praising both performance and audio quality; only a handful of minor critiques (Phil’s bass on one review). The weighted average of show (≈5) and recording (≈4.7) yields ~4.8.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix, balanced vocals/instruments, no noticeable hiss or drop‑outs; a few reviewers note occasional cut‑outs on specific tracks but overall the SBD is described as “perfect” and “as good as any SHN.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set one builds steadily with rockers and tight jams, leading into a high‑octane second set where China>Rider launches an extended groove that carries through Playing, Uncle John’s, and a soaring space before a magical encore. Reviewers note superb transitions (e.g., Shakedown → Franklin’s) and a consistently energetic pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for scorching solos (Row Jimmy, China Rider), powerful tone, and infectious stage energy; described as “on fire” and the emotional heart of the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Generally lauded for a solid bass foundation; one reviewer felt his presence was weaker on this recording but most note his “tight” playing throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals highlighted as crisp and front‑center; occasional minor flub noted, but overall praised for strong singing (especially on The Music Never Stopped).
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ work singled out as “rocking,” “the best of the Brent years,” with lush fills that lift the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart praised for tight, energetic drumming; “drumz” segment described as “beautiful” and a catalyst for the second‑set fire.
